SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS TO BE FILED TOGETHER WITH A PROPOSED ORDER ON THE QUESTION (ASSUMING THE 2006 DEFAULT IS INVALID, IS THE 2010 DEFAULT INVALID AND DID DEFENDANT ACT WITH SUFFICIENT DILIGENCE IN SEEKING TO VACATE IT ?) LAW AND MOTION 302, NTC OF MTN AND MTN TO DISMISS PLTF'S COMPLAINT; MEMO OF P & A; DEC AND POS. THE COURT TRIED TO CONTACT MS. ZUSCHIN THROUGH THE PHONE NUMBERS ON THE RECORD AND ON HER OPPOSITION PAPERS. THERE WAS NO RESPONSE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT THE AMELIA CHAN YEE 2004 LIVING TRUST, BY AND THROUGH ITS TRUSTEE, AMELIA CHAN YEE'S MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THIS ACTION IS DISMISSED PURSUANT TO CCP 583.310 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILED TO BRING THIS ACTION TO TRIAL WITHIN 5 YEARS AFTER IT WAS COMMENCED. THE OVER FOUR YEARS BETWEEN THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND THE ENTRY OF THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS NOT EXCLUDED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXPLAIN WHY IT TOOK HER SO LONG TO OBTAIN THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT. (HUGHES V. KIMBLE (1992) 5 CAL.APP.4TH 59). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: ANGIE DINER CSR # 9581. (302)

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Pltf'S Complaint; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
Off Calendar May-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Scott C. Stratman, Esq., of Stratman Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 510.457.3465, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
No other appearance.

The court tried to contact Ms. Zuschin through the phone numbers on the record and on her opposition papers. There was no response. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE AMELIA CHAN YEE 2004 LIVING TRUST, BY AND THROUGH ITS TRUSTEE, AMELIA CHAN YEE'S MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT is Granted. This action is dismissed pursuant to CCP 583.310 because plaintiff failed to bring this action to trial within 5 years after it was commenced. The over four years between the entry of default and the entry of the default judgment is not excluded because plaintiff failed to explain why it took her so long to obtain the default judgment. (Hughes v. Kimble (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 59). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Angie Diner CSR # 9581

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar Jul-27-2011 Denied

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-27-2011 Continued To Feb-22-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT CAPLE STRATMAN OF THE VALERIAN, PATTERSON & STRATMAN, TEL. NO. (510)521-0612 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - DEFENDANTS THE AMELIA CHAN YEE 2004 LIVING TRUST, BY AND THROGH ITS TRUSTEE, AMELIA CHAN YEE'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 22, 2011 FOR HEARING ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ALLOWED SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFING ON THIS MATTER SINCE NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY THE PLAINTIFF. SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS TO BE FILED TOGETHER WITH A PROPOSED ORDER ON THE QUESTION (ASSUMING THE 2006 DEFAULT IS INVALID, IS THE 2010 DEFAULT INVALID AND DID DEFENDANT ACT WITH SUFFICIENT DILIGENCE IN SEEKING TO VACATE IT ?) ADDRESSED ON THE TENTATIVE RULING TO BE FILED NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 15 WITH COURTESY COPIES TO BE DELIVERED IN DEPARTMENT 301 BY 2/15/11. MR. STRATMAN TO PROVIDE NEW NOTICE ON THE COURT'S DIRECTION. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
